description:
The Skating Club of Boston, located at 1240 Providence Highway in Norwood, Massachusetts, stands as one of the most prestigious figure skating clubs in the United States and a beacon for competitive ice skating. Founded in 1912, this legendary institution has produced numerous Olympic and World champions, most notably Nancy Kerrigan, along with countless other elite skaters who have represented the United States on the international stage. Operating two sheets of ice, the Skating Club of Boston combines elite-level figure skating training with accessible recreational opportunities for skaters of all levels.

The facility serves as a comprehensive hub for ice skating disciplines, offering intensive coaching programs for competitive figure skaters, hockey development, and public skating sessions for recreational enthusiasts. The rink has established a notable partnership with Figure Skating in Harlem, extending ice skating opportunities to underrepresented communities. Whether you're a serious competitive skater pursuing national rankings or a family seeking weekend public skating, the Skating Club of Boston welcomes all skill levels while maintaining its reputation as a serious competitive training environment.

Located conveniently in Norwood (relocated from its original Boston location), the facility provides excellent access to the MetroWest Boston area and surrounding communities. The presence of top-tier coaching staff and competitive programs makes this rink an attractive destination for skaters seeking world-class instruction and training. Public skating sessions offer visitors the opportunity to skate on the same ice that has hosted championship athletes, making it a special experience for ice skating enthusiasts throughout Massachusetts.
what_to_know: Check scboston.org for current public skating schedules, which vary by season and may be limited during competitive training sessions
The facility operates two sheets of ice; confirm which sheet hosts public skating before your visit
Lessons and figure skating programs range from beginner to competitive levels; advance registration is typically required
Skate rentals are available; bring your own skates if you prefer or prefer a specific fit
The facility's prestigious reputation means peak times fill quickly—arrive early for public sessions, especially weekends
Parking is available on-site; the location is accessible via local roads from Boston and surrounding areas
The rink hosts skating camps and special programming throughout the year; check the website for seasonal offerings

faq.question: Does the Skating Club of Boston offer public skating?
faq.answer: Yes, the Skating Club of Boston welcomes recreational skaters during designated public skating sessions. Hours and availability vary seasonally and may be limited during competitive training periods, so check their website at scboston.org for current schedules before visiting.

faq.question: Can I take figure skating lessons at the Skating Club of Boston?
faq.answer: The Skating Club of Boston offers figure skating instruction from beginner through competitive levels. Lessons are available through the club's coaching staff, and interested skaters should contact the facility directly or visit scboston.org to inquire about lesson availability and registration.

faq.question: How is the Skating Club of Boston connected to Nancy Kerrigan?
faq.answer: Nancy Kerrigan, the 1994 Olympic silver medalist, trained at the Skating Club of Boston as a competitive figure skater. The club has produced numerous Olympic and World champions throughout its history and remains one of the premier figure skating training facilities in the United States.

faq.question: What is the difference between the two sheets of ice at the facility?
faq.answer: The Skating Club of Boston operates two sheets of ice to accommodate its high volume of competitive training programs, coaching, and public sessions. Public skating is typically hosted on one sheet while the other may host competitive or training sessions; confirm which sheet is available for public use when you plan your visit.

faq.question: Does the Skating Club of Boston offer hockey programs?
faq.answer: While the Skating Club of Boston is primarily known for figure skating excellence, the facility does support ice hockey programming. Contact the club directly at scboston.org or by phone to learn about current hockey team opportunities, competitive leagues, and learn-to-play programs.

description:
The New England Sports Center in Marlborough, Massachusetts, ranks among the largest and most comprehensive ice skating complexes in all of New England, featuring an impressive six sheets of ice. Located at 1 Sports Center Road, this expansive facility serves the MetroWest Boston area and beyond, hosting public skating, competitive figure skating programs, youth and adult hockey leagues, and major regional and national ice hockey tournaments. The NESC's multiple sheets of ice and extensive amenities make it the premier destination for serious hockey programs and elite figure skating training throughout Massachusetts and southern New England.

The facility's diverse programming accommodates every segment of the ice skating community, from families enjoying recreational public skating sessions to competitive athletes training for national championships. With six sheets of ice operating simultaneously, the New England Sports Center manages one of the highest volumes of ice sports programming in the region, hosting prestigious hockey tournaments that draw teams from across New England and beyond. Figure skating clubs operate on-site, providing coaching and competitive pathways for aspiring champions, while public skating sessions offer accessible recreation for casual skaters and families.

Beyond ice activities, the New England Sports Center features a large pro shop stocked with skating equipment, apparel, and accessories, plus multiple concession areas to fuel your skating adventure. The facility's modern infrastructure, professional staff, and central MetroWest location make it an ideal choice for anyone seeking comprehensive ice skating services. Whether you're registering your child in learn-to-skate programs, competing in regional hockey tournaments, or enjoying family public skating, the NESC provides exceptional facilities and programming year-round.
what_to_know: Visit nesc.com for complete public skating schedules, as times vary across six sheets and change seasonally
The facility hosts major youth and regional hockey tournaments throughout the year; check the website for tournament dates if you plan to watch or participate
Six sheets of ice mean NESC hosts simultaneous programming; call ahead to confirm availability and any schedule changes
The pro shop on-site offers skate rentals, sales, and professional fitting services for optimal comfort
Concession areas provide food and beverages; bring cash or be prepared for card payments
Ample parking is available on the grounds; the facility is easily accessible from the MetroWest region and Routes 9 and 290
Learn-to-skate programs fill quickly, especially in fall and winter; register early for youth and adult programs

faq.question: How many sheets of ice does the New England Sports Center have?
faq.answer: The New England Sports Center operates six sheets of ice, making it one of the largest ice skating complexes in New England. This allows the facility to host multiple programs, public skating sessions, figure skating training, and major hockey tournaments simultaneously throughout the year.

faq.question: What tournaments does the NESC host?
faq.answer: The New England Sports Center hosts youth hockey tournaments, regional tournaments, and national-level ice hockey championships throughout the season. Check nesc.com for the current tournament schedule, as events vary by season and may require advance registration or ticketing.

faq.question: Are figure skating programs available at NESC?
faq.answer: Yes, the New England Sports Center hosts figure skating clubs and offers coaching programs for figure skaters of various levels. Visit nesc.com or contact the facility directly to learn about specific figure skating coaching options, learn-to-skate programs, and competitive pathways available.

faq.question: Does NESC offer skate rentals and a pro shop?
faq.answer: The New England Sports Center features a full-service pro shop offering skate rentals, sales, professional fitting, apparel, and accessories. The shop staff can help you find properly fitting rental skates or assist with equipment purchases for your skating needs.

faq.question: What are the public skating hours at the New England Sports Center?
faq.answer: Public skating hours vary by sheet and season at the NESC. Visit nesc.com or call the facility directly to confirm current public skating schedules, as six sheets hosting simultaneous programming means times may vary throughout the week and throughout the season.

description:
Steriti Memorial Rink, located at 561 Commercial Street in Boston's historic North End and waterfront district, serves as an accessible, affordable public ice skating facility operated by the City of Boston's Department of Conservation and Recreation. This single-sheet neighborhood rink provides essential ice skating services to the North End, Charlestown, downtown Boston, and surrounding communities, combining recreational public skating with hockey leagues and comprehensive learn-to-skate programming. Strategically positioned near Boston's waterfront attractions, Steriti Memorial Rink offers visitors an opportunity to enjoy quality ice skating in the heart of historic Boston.

The facility's programming emphasizes accessibility and community engagement, offering public skating sessions throughout the week and weekend for families, casual skaters, and ice hockey enthusiasts. Learn-to-skate programs serve beginning skaters of all ages, including children and adults seeking foundational instruction and technique development. Youth and adult hockey leagues operate at the rink, fostering competitive ice hockey development within the North End and surrounding neighborhoods while maintaining welcoming recreational opportunities for the broader public.

As part of the DCR (Department of Conservation and Recreation) Metropolitan District Commission rink system, Steriti Memorial Rink represents Boston's commitment to providing affordable public ice skating access to urban residents and visitors. The rink's proximity to the Boston waterfront, historic cobblestone streets, Haymarket restaurants, and downtown attractions makes it an ideal addition to a Boston visit. Affordable public skating rates make this facility an excellent choice for families seeking budget-friendly ice skating in the city.
what_to_know: Call or visit the DCR website for current public skating schedules, as times may vary seasonally and may change for hockey programming
Parking is limited in the North End; consider using nearby paid lots, street parking, or arriving via the Green or Orange Line T access
The facility is located on Commercial Street; nearby attractions include the Rose Kennedy Greenway, the waterfront, and historic North End restaurants
Skate rentals are available at modest rates; bring your own skates if you prefer
Arrive early during peak weekend times, as the single-sheet facility fills quickly with neighborhood hockey players and public skaters
Learn-to-skate programs are offered for children and adults; check the website for current registration and class schedules
Amenities are basic but functional; bring water and snacks or plan to grab food at nearby North End establishments

faq.question: Where is Steriti Memorial Rink located?
faq.answer: Steriti Memorial Rink is located at 561 Commercial Street in Boston's North End neighborhood, near the waterfront and historic attractions like the Rose Kennedy Greenway and Faneuil Hall area. It's accessible via the Green or Orange Line T and nearby paid parking.

faq.question: Is Steriti Memorial Rink affordable for public skating?
faq.answer: Yes, Steriti Memorial Rink is a City of Boston DCR-operated public facility with affordable public skating rates, making it an excellent value option for families and casual skaters seeking quality ice skating at reasonable prices in Boston proper.

faq.question: What programs does Steriti offer beyond public skating?
faq.answer: Steriti Memorial Rink offers hockey leagues for youth and adults, learn-to-skate programs for beginning skaters of all ages, and competitive ice hockey development programming. Check the DCR website or call for current program schedules and registration information.

faq.question: How do I get to Steriti Memorial Rink using public transportation?
faq.answer: Steriti Memorial Rink is accessible via the MBTA Green Line (Haymarket or North Station stops) and Orange Line (North Station). The facility is located on Commercial Street in the North End, a short walk from these transit stations.

faq.question: Can I rent skates at Steriti Memorial Rink?
faq.answer: Yes, skate rentals are available at Steriti Memorial Rink at affordable rates. Bring your own skates if you prefer, or inquire at the rental desk for sizing and availability when you arrive.

description:
Bajko Memorial Rink, situated at 5 Cummins Highway in the Roslindale neighborhood of Boston, serves as a vital community ice skating facility operated by the City of Boston's Department of Conservation and Recreation. This single-sheet neighborhood rink provides affordable public skating, hockey programming, and learn-to-skate instruction to the Roslindale, West Roxbury, Jamaica Plain, and surrounding Boston communities. As part of the DCR Metropolitan District Commission rink system, Bajko Memorial Rink embodies Boston's commitment to providing accessible ice skating opportunities in neighborhood settings throughout the city.

The facility offers comprehensive programming designed to serve families, recreational skaters, and hockey players of all skill levels. Public skating sessions throughout the week and weekend provide community members with accessible ice skating opportunities, while learn-to-skate programs introduce beginning skaters to fundamental skating techniques and confidence-building. Youth and adult hockey leagues develop ice hockey talent within the neighborhood while fostering community engagement through recreational league play.

Bajko Memorial Rink's location in the Roslindale neighborhood places it at the heart of a vibrant Boston residential community with convenient street parking, nearby shops, and local amenities. The facility's affordable pricing and neighborhood focus make it a preferred choice for South Boston families seeking quality ice skating without traveling to distant facilities. Whether you're introducing children to ice skating or maintaining your own recreational skating skills, Bajko Memorial Rink provides essential ice skating access to Boston's West Side communities.

Street parking is available in the Roslindale neighborhood; arrive early during peak times to secure a convenient spot
Skate rentals are offered at budget-friendly rates; bring your own skates if you prefer a better fit
The facility is a single sheet; hockey practices and games may occasionally adjust public skating times
Learn-to-skate programs serve children and adults; check the DCR website for current registration, class levels, and seasonal schedules
Nearby Roslindale Square offers restaurants and shops within walking distance
The rink is easily accessible via Route 9 and local roads; bring water and snacks as on-site amenities are limited

faq.question: Is Bajko Memorial Rink in Boston or a suburb?
faq.answer: Bajko Memorial Rink is located in Roslindale, a neighborhood within Boston proper. It's situated at 5 Cummins Highway and serves the Roslindale, West Roxbury, and Jamaica Plain neighborhoods as a City of Boston DCR-operated facility.

faq.question: How much does public skating cost at Bajko?
faq.answer: Bajko Memorial Rink is a City of Boston DCR facility with affordable public skating rates. Specific pricing varies seasonally; check the DCR website or call the facility directly for current public skating admission fees.

faq.question: Does Bajko Memorial Rink have hockey programs?
faq.answer: Yes, Bajko Memorial Rink hosts youth and adult hockey leagues, providing opportunities for recreational and competitive ice hockey development. Contact the facility or check the DCR website for current hockey team registrations, league schedules, and learn-to-play options.

faq.answer: Bajko Memorial Rink offers learn-to-skate programs for children and adults seeking to develop foundational skating skills. Check the DCR website or contact the facility for current program levels, class schedules, and registration information.

faq.question: Can I find parking near Bajko Memorial Rink?
faq.answer: Street parking is available throughout the Roslindale neighborhood near Bajko Memorial Rink at 5 Cummins Highway. Arrive early during peak skating times to secure a convenient spot, and be prepared to search the surrounding residential streets if the immediate area is full.

description:
Hobomock Arena, located at 404 Church Street in Pembroke, Massachusetts, serves as a family-friendly ice skating destination for the South Shore region. This single-sheet facility provides comprehensive ice skating programming including public skating, figure skating instruction and clubs, and youth ice hockey development to the communities of Pembroke, Duxbury, Hanover, Plymouth, and surrounding South Shore towns. Hobomock Arena has established itself as the regional hub for ice skating activities, offering welcoming recreational opportunities alongside competitive figure skating and hockey programs that attract serious athletes throughout southeastern Massachusetts.

The arena's diverse programming addresses the entire spectrum of ice skating interests and skill levels. Public skating sessions throughout the week provide families and casual skaters with accessible recreation, while figure skating clubs offer coaching and competitive pathways for ambitious skaters. Strong youth hockey programs develop young players through learn-to-play classes, recreational leagues, and competitive team opportunities, fostering ice hockey talent within the South Shore community. The facility's emphasis on youth programming and family engagement makes it a central gathering place for ice skating enthusiasts throughout the region.

Located in the charming South Shore town of Pembroke, Hobomock Arena provides convenient access to the region's growing ice skating community without requiring travel to Boston-area facilities. The facility's commitment to affordability and accessibility aligns with South Shore values, making quality ice skating available to families throughout the area. With strong connections to the Pembroke, Duxbury, Hanover, and Plymouth communities, Hobomock Arena represents the regional growth and enthusiasm for ice skating in southeastern Massachusetts.

The facility is a single sheet; hockey practices and games may occasionally affect public skating availability

Figure skating coaching is available; inquire about lesson availability and club coaching opportunities when you visit

Youth hockey teams represent communities throughout the South Shore; check the website or call for tryout dates and team registration information

faq.question: What communities does Hobomock Arena serve?
faq.answer: Hobomock Arena serves the South Shore region including Pembroke, Duxbury, Hanover, Plymouth, and surrounding communities. The facility has become the regional hub for ice skating activities, offering convenient access to public skating, figure skating, and hockey programs for South Shore residents.

faq.question: Does Hobomock Arena have figure skating programs?
faq.answer: Yes, Hobomock Arena hosts figure skating clubs and offers coaching instruction for figure skaters of various levels. faq.answer: Hobomock Arena is located at 404 Church Street in Pembroke, Massachusetts. The facility is easily accessible via Route 3 and local South Shore roads, with ample parking available on-site for visitors from throughout the region.

description:
Canton Sportsplex, situated at 850 Washington Street in Canton, Massachusetts, represents a multi-sport recreational complex that integrates an ice skating rink with additional athletic facilities. The single-sheet ice arena provides public skating, figure skating instruction, and youth and adult ice hockey programming to the residents of Canton, Stoughton, Sharon, and the broader south suburban Boston region. As a combined sports facility, Canton Sportsplex offers convenience for families seeking diverse recreational activities in one location, with the ice rink serving as a cornerstone of the complex's athletic programming.

The facility's ice skating operations emphasize family engagement and recreational access, offering public skating sessions designed to introduce newcomers to ice skating while accommodating experienced skaters seeking regular recreational skating opportunities. Learn-to-skate programs serve children and adults, providing foundational instruction and technique development in a welcoming environment. Youth hockey leagues develop young hockey talent within Canton and surrounding south suburban communities, fostering competitive play and recreational participation opportunities for players of various skill levels.

Canton Sportsplex's integrated approach to sports and recreation makes it a one-stop destination for families seeking diverse athletic activities and ice skating opportunities. Located in the town of Canton with convenient access from Routes 138, 1, and 128, the facility serves the growing suburban population south of Boston. The combination of ice skating with other recreational facilities creates an attractive value proposition for families balancing multiple children's athletic interests and parental fitness goals.

The ice rink is a single sheet; hockey leagues and programs may occasionally affect public skating availability

Learn-to-skate programs for children and adults are offered; check the website for current class schedules and registration
The facility offers other sports and recreation amenities beyond ice skating; inquire about combined facility memberships or packages

Figure skating coaching and instruction may be available; contact the facility to ask about coaching options and lesson availability

faq.question: What is Canton Sportsplex beyond the ice rink?

faq.answer: Canton Sportsplex is located at 850 Washington Street in Canton, Massachusetts, south of Boston. The facility is easily accessible via Routes 138, 1, and 128, serving the Canton, Stoughton, Sharon, and surrounding south suburban communities.

description:
Valley Sports Arena, located at 126 Baker Avenue in Concord, Massachusetts, stands as a historic and established ice skating facility serving the MetroWest and Route 495 corridor communities. This single-sheet arena offers comprehensive programming including public skating, figure skating instruction and clubs, and hockey leagues spanning youth through adult levels. Valley Sports Arena has built a strong reputation within the Concord, Acton, Sudbury, and broader 495 belt region for fostering ice skating talent and providing accessible recreational opportunities to suburban families seeking quality ice skating in the MetroWest area.

The facility's programming philosophy emphasizes community engagement and recreational access alongside competitive development. Public skating sessions throughout the week provide families and casual skaters with regular ice skating opportunities, while figure skating coaching and club programs develop competitive skaters pursuing local, state, and regional championships. Adult hockey leagues offer recreational play opportunities for working adults seeking league competition and social engagement, while youth hockey programs develop young talent through learn-to-play, recreational, and competitive pathways.

Valley Sports Arena's location in historic Concord places it at the gateway of the Route 495 tech corridor, serving an affluent suburban region with strong youth sports participation and family engagement. The facility's established reputation, strong coaching staff, and community atmosphere make it a preferred destination for MetroWest families. Whether you're seeking introductory skating lessons, regular recreational play, or competitive coaching, Valley Sports Arena provides experienced facilities and professional programming rooted in decades of ice skating tradition.
what_to_know: Call or check local sources for current public skating schedules, as times vary seasonally and may be affected by league play
Strong adult hockey leagues operate at the facility; inquire about recreational league registration and play opportunities
Figure skating coaching is available; contact the arena for information about lesson availability, club coaching, and competitive pathways
Learn-to-skate programs for children serve the broader 495 community; register early as programs fill during fall and winter
The facility is a single sheet; hockey games and practices may occasionally affect public skating availability
Convenient parking is available on-site; the location is accessible from Concord center and Route 495 corridor roads
Skate rentals are available; bring your own skates if you prefer or prefer a specific style

faq.question: How long has Valley Sports Arena been operating?
faq.answer: Valley Sports Arena is a historic, well-established ice skating facility in Concord that has served the MetroWest and Route 495 corridor communities for many years. The arena is known for its strong reputation for developing skating talent and providing quality recreational opportunities.

faq.question: What adult hockey opportunities are available at Valley Sports Arena?
faq.answer: Valley Sports Arena hosts recreational adult hockey leagues offering play opportunities for working adults seeking league competition and social engagement. Contact the facility or check the website for current league registration, team information, and play schedules.

faq.question: Does Valley Sports Arena offer figure skating instruction?
faq.answer: Yes, Valley Sports Arena provides figure skating coaching and hosts figure skating clubs for skaters of various competitive levels. Contact the facility to inquire about coaching availability, lesson options, and club membership information.

faq.answer: Yes, Valley Sports Arena offers learn-to-skate programs for children in various age groups and skill levels. Check with the facility or local Concord recreation department for current program schedules, registration dates, and instruction information.

description:
Brighton Arena, situated at 218 North Beacon Street in the Brighton neighborhood of Boston, operates as a historic and iconic city ice skating facility within the Department of Conservation and Recreation system. This single-sheet neighborhood rink provides affordable public skating, hockey programming, and learn-to-skate instruction to the residents of Brighton, Allston, Newton, and the Boston University and Boston College communities. As one of Boston's historic neighborhood rinks, Brighton Arena embodies the city's legacy of accessible ice skating and serves as a community anchor for the western neighborhoods of Boston and adjacent areas.

Brighton Arena's programming prioritizes affordability and community access, offering public skating sessions throughout the week and weekend at minimal cost. Youth and adult hockey leagues develop ice hockey talent within the neighborhood while maintaining welcoming recreational participation opportunities for players of all abilities. Learn-to-skate programs introduce beginning skaters to fundamental skating techniques in a supportive community environment, with instruction available for children and adults seeking foundational skills and confidence building. The facility's central location within the Brighton neighborhood makes it a natural gathering place for local families.

Located on North Beacon Street with convenient access via the MBTA Green Line B branch and local roads, Brighton Arena provides essential ice skating services to Boston's western neighborhoods and the university communities of Boston University and Boston College. The facility's historic significance, affordable pricing, and accessible transit connections make it an ideal choice for students, families, and longtime neighborhood residents seeking quality ice skating in Boston proper. Whether you're a student seeking recreational skating or a neighborhood family introducing children to ice skating, Brighton Arena offers authentic Boston ice skating tradition.
what_to_know: Check the DCR website or call for current public skating schedules, as hours vary seasonally and may be affected by hockey programming
The Green Line B branch of the MBTA provides convenient access; the facility is walking distance from Brighton Avenue stops
Street parking is available in the Brighton neighborhood; arrive early during peak weekend times for convenient parking
Skate rentals are offered at affordable rates; bring your own skates if you prefer
Hockey leagues and recreational programs serve youth and adults; inquire about team registrations and league schedules
Learn-to-skate programs serve children and adults; check the DCR website for current program levels and registration
Nearby Brighton Avenue offers restaurants, shops, and other neighborhood businesses within walking distance

faq.question: How do I get to Brighton Arena using the MBTA?
faq.answer: Brighton Arena is located at 218 North Beacon Street in Brighton and is accessible via the MBTA Green Line B branch, which serves the Brighton Avenue area. The facility is within walking distance of several Green Line B stops, making it convenient for Boston residents and university students.

faq.question: Is Brighton Arena affordable for students and families?
faq.answer: Yes, Brighton Arena is a City of Boston DCR-operated facility with affordable public skating rates, making it an excellent value option for students, families, and neighborhood residents seeking quality ice skating at reasonable prices.

faq.question: What programs serve the Boston University and Boston College communities?
faq.answer: Brighton Arena's location near Boston University and Boston College makes it convenient for students seeking recreational ice skating and hockey participation. faq.question: Are hockey leagues available at Brighton Arena?
faq.answer: Yes, Brighton Arena hosts youth and adult hockey leagues providing recreational and competitive opportunities for ice hockey players. Contact the facility or check the DCR website for current team registrations, league schedules, and registration deadlines.

faq.question: Why is Brighton Arena considered historic?
faq.answer: Brighton Arena is one of Boston's historic neighborhood rinks, part of the city's legacy of providing accessible ice skating to urban residents. The facility has served the Brighton, Allston, and Newton communities for many years and remains an iconic gathering place for ice skating tradition.
